metadata.name: Continuous Easy Run
metadata.description: Default continuous easy or recovery running with no structured fast work.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum continuous easy duration from duration_minutes floor.

display.description_template: An easy, conversational run. Keep the effort relaxed enough that you could speak in full sentences the whole way. Do not chase pace today.

coach_notes.intended_use: Default filler for non-quality running days, recovery days, and rolling adjustment downgrades.
special_rules: Easy runs are always prescribed by duration, never by pace.
Distance range is displayed after duration using the athlete's easy pace range.
Recovery variant is capped at 30 minutes.
No RPE collected for standard easy runs.
HR zone 1–2 compliance is monitored passively.

metadata.name: Easy Run with Strides
metadata.description: Easy running with short relaxed accelerations to maintain coordination, turnover, and running economy.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum session duration remains the easy-run floor; strides occur inside the final stride window.

display.description_template: Run easily throughout, then finish with {{stride_count}} relaxed strides. Accelerate smoothly, stay controlled, and focus on quick, efficient running rather than sprinting.

coach_notes.intended_use: Neuromuscular preparation before workouts and races. Maintains economy and turnover without adding meaningful fatigue.
special_rules: Target 1–2 stride sessions per week.
Prefer the day before workouts.
Prefer the day before races.
Avoid the day after workouts.
Strides are controlled accelerations, not sprints.
Full recovery between strides.
Can be performed on road, track, grass, or flat trail.

metadata.name: Continuous Long Run
metadata.description: Continuous aerobic long run performed entirely at easy effort.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum continuous long-run duration from duration_minutes floor.

display.description_template: A steady aerobic long run. Stay relaxed, keep the effort controlled, and focus on accumulating time on your feet.

coach_notes.intended_use: Foundational aerobic long run. Primary long-run archetype during base phase and development plans.
special_rules: Long-run duration derived from weekly volume target.
Target duration is approximately 28% of weekly running volume.
Absolute minimum duration is 60 minutes.
No embedded quality work.
No separate warmup or cooldown segments.

metadata.name: Progression Long Run
metadata.description: Long run that gradually increases in intensity throughout the run.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum progression long-run duration from duration_minutes floor.

display.description_template: Start comfortably and gradually increase the effort throughout the run. Finish strong and controlled without forcing the pace.

coach_notes.intended_use: Bridge between purely aerobic long runs and long runs with embedded quality.
special_rules: Progression is continuous rather than segmented.
Finish zone determined by phase and goal distance.
5K athletes may progress closer to threshold.
Marathon athletes may finish closer to marathon effort.
No separate warmup or cooldown segments.

metadata.name: Long Run with Goal Pace Segments
metadata.description: Long run containing structured blocks at goal race pace.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Long-run archetype; minimum footprint follows long-run absolute floor until segment-specific floors are added.

display.title_template: Long Run with Goal Pace Segments
display.description_template: A long run with structured segments at goal race pace. Focus on rhythm, efficiency, and learning how race effort feels late in a run.

coach_notes.intended_use: Race-specific long run used primarily during build and peak phases.
special_rules: Most common for half marathon and marathon athletes.
May be used sparingly for 10K athletes.
Rare for 5K athletes.
Can generate continuous, broken, or hybrid structures.
Goal pace volume expands throughout the cycle.

metadata.name: Fast Finish Long Run
metadata.description: Aerobic long run followed by a substantial faster closing segment.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum fast-finish long-run duration from duration_minutes floor.

display.description_template: Run aerobically for most of the session, then close with a strong final segment. Finish controlled and efficient rather than all-out.

coach_notes.intended_use: Develops fatigue resistance and teaches athletes to run well late in a race.
special_rules: Distinguished from progression runs by a clear late-run gear shift.
Finish intensity determined by phase, goal distance, and athlete classification.
Most common for half marathon and marathon athletes.
Can be used sparingly for 10K athletes.
No separate warmup or cooldown.

metadata.name: Structured Fartlek Ladder
metadata.description: Time-based fartlek session using ascending, descending, or symmetric ladders of faster running and easy recoveries.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Canonical minimum for a viable ladder session across variants: warmup/cooldown plus a coach-meaningful ladder main set with jog recoveries.

display.description_template: {{round_count}} x {{fartlek_ladder_sequence}} fartlek ladder with equal easy recovery. Run the longer efforts controlled and the shorter efforts a little quicker. Recover easily between each one.

coach_notes.intended_use: Flexible quality session for base, development, maintenance, and lighter build/peak work. Useful when the athlete needs quality without the rigidity of track intervals.
special_rules: Fartlek remains time-based even though it is a quality session.
Can serve as primary or secondary quality.
Recovery is generated from interval duration using the fartlek recovery model.
Longer efforts should generally map to 10K effort; shorter efforts may progress toward 5K or mile effort.
Sharp descending variant should be used more often in build/peak and less often for lower-volume workable athletes.

metadata.name: Equal Distance Repeats
metadata.description: Track or measured-distance intervals using repeated reps of identical distance.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Estimated from minimum quality volume at assigned repeat effort, jog recoveries, 15 min warmup, and 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{rep_count}} x {{rep_distance_meters}}m. Run each repeat at a consistent controlled effort. {{repeat_consistency_instruction}}

coach_notes.intended_use: Foundational interval archetype for aerobic power, VO2 development, and race-specific fitness.
special_rules: Progress primarily by increasing total interval volume.
Generator may increase reps, rep distance, or both.
Recovery generated from the vo2_standard recovery model.
Track preferred but road/GPS versions are acceptable.
Target effort determined by phase and goal distance.
Warmup includes strides by default.

metadata.name: Mixed Distance Repeats
metadata.description: Interval sessions combining multiple repeat distances and/or effort zones within the same workout.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Estimated from minimum mixed-repeat quality volume at assigned efforts, jog recoveries, 15 min warmup, and 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: A mixed-distance interval session that combines different repeat lengths and speeds. Stay controlled early and maintain quality throughout the workout.

coach_notes.intended_use: Advanced interval archetype used to create variety, race specificity, and multiple physiological stimuli within one session.
special_rules: Can generate classic ladder workouts.
Can generate strength-plus-speed sessions.
Can generate speed-plus-strength sessions.
More common in peak phase.
Track preferred but road equivalents may be generated.
1600m segments follow the same eligibility rules as equal_distance_repeats.

metadata.name: Short Speed Repeats
metadata.description: Short repetitions focused on economy, turnover, speed development, and speed endurance.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Estimated from minimum speed volume, generous movement recoveries, 15 min warmup, and 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{rep_count}} × {{rep_distance_meters}}m at near-sprint effort. Run each rep fast and controlled, powerful but not falling apart. Take full walk-back recovery between reps to preserve speed and mechanics on every one.

coach_notes.intended_use: Develop economy, turnover, speed, and speed endurance without excessive metabolic stress.
special_rules: Recovery is intentionally generous.
Form quality is prioritized over fatigue.
400m repetitions may appear here when the stimulus is speed endurance rather than aerobic interval work.
Most heavily weighted for 5K athletes.
Can be used as a secondary quality session.

metadata.name: Sustained Hill Repeats
metadata.description: Time-based uphill repeats on a sustained runnable hill with jog-back recovery and optional checkpoint standing rest.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum viable hill session: 15 min warmup + 4 x 45 sec uphill with equal jog-back recovery + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{rep_count}} x {{rep_duration_seconds}} sec uphill on a runnable 4-8% hill. Run each uphill repeat at the assigned effort while keeping your mechanics strong. Jog back down easily after each rep. {{checkpoint_recovery_instruction}}

coach_notes.intended_use: Builds strength, aerobic power, and climbing mechanics without requiring track access.
special_rules: Prescribed by time, not distance.
Hill grade guidance is recommended but not mandatory.
Effort is mapped by goal distance and rep duration.
Shorter reps may map to faster race-distance efforts; longer reps map to more controlled efforts.
Jog-back recovery is standard.
Optional checkpoint standing recovery appears around 25%, 50%, and 75% of the session, or halfway if there are too few reps.
Suitable as primary or secondary quality depending on phase and athlete load.

metadata.name: Hill Sprints
metadata.description: Short, steep hill sprints for neuromuscular power, force production, and running economy.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum viable hill sprint session: 20 min warmup + 6 x 8 sec sprints with full walk-back recovery + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{sprint_count}} x {{sprint_duration_seconds}} sec uphill sprints on a short, steep hill. Sprint uphill hard but controlled. {{sprint_recovery_instruction}} These are about power and mechanics, not getting tired.

coach_notes.intended_use: Neuromuscular speed and power development for all race distances.
special_rules: Hill sprints are allowed for workable athletes.
Full recovery is required.
Do not turn this into an aerobic workout.
Stop the session if mechanics deteriorate.
Best used as secondary quality or attached to an otherwise easy day.
Avoid the day after a workout and avoid the day before races.

metadata.name: Plyometric Hill Circuits
metadata.description: Short hill circuits combining uphill running with plyometric and coordination drills.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum viable plyometric hill circuit session with warmup, cooldown, and movement recovery between circuits.

display.description_template: Complete a series of short uphill efforts combined with plyometric drills. Focus on coordination, stiffness, rhythm, and quality movement. Recover fully between circuits.

coach_notes.intended_use: Develop elastic strength, coordination, and running economy through low-volume, high-quality plyometric work.
special_rules: Coach clearance required.
Plyometric clearance required on athlete profile.
Never prescribe during return-to-running plans.
Quality of movement is more important than volume.
Stop the session if mechanics deteriorate.
Use full recovery between circuits.
Best suited for base and early build phases.
Can be substituted with flat-ground plyometric circuits if hill access is unavailable.

metadata.name: Tempo Intervals
metadata.description: Threshold training performed in multiple sustained segments separated by short recoveries.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum tempo interval session: 15 min warmup + 2 x 6 min tempo + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{rep_count}} x {{rep_duration_minutes}} min tempo reps at comfortably hard effort. {{tempo_recovery_instruction}}

coach_notes.intended_use: Primary threshold-development workout across all goal distances.
special_rules: Time-based prescriptions are preferred.
Distance-based prescriptions remain available.
Recovery is generated using the threshold_standard model.
Primary progression occurs through threshold volume expansion.
One of the highest-frequency quality archetypes in the system.
Warmup includes strides by default.

metadata.name: Continuous Progression Tempo
metadata.description: Continuous aerobic and threshold work performed without recovery breaks.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum progression tempo: 15 min warmup + 20 min continuous work + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: Warm up {{warmup_minutes}} min easy. {{progression_instruction}} Cool down {{cooldown_minutes}} min easy.

coach_notes.intended_use: Continuous threshold development and aerobic strength without stopping or resetting effort.
special_rules: No recovery breaks are allowed.
Goal-distance-adjusted effort progression.
If the session uses easy recoveries, use structured_fartlek_ladder instead.
Wave variants are encouraged for well-trained athletes.
Float running should be favored over easy running when appropriate.
One of the primary development-plan quality archetypes.
Warmup includes strides by default.

metadata.name: Long Run with Pickups
metadata.description: Long aerobic run with short time-based pickups inserted throughout the run.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum pickup long-run duration from duration_minutes floor; pickups occur within the long run.

display.description_template: Run the long run mostly at easy effort. Insert {{pickup_count}} pickups of {{pickup_duration_seconds}} seconds throughout the run, returning to easy effort after each one. The pickups should create rhythm changes without turning the run into a workout.

coach_notes.intended_use: Introduces rhythm changes, economy work, and light fatigue resistance inside a long run without making the long run race-specific.
special_rules: Pickups are generally time-based.
Distance-based pickups are allowed but should be uncommon.
Pickup effort ranges from steady to 5K depending on duration, phase, goal distance, and classification.
Athlete should return to easy effort between pickups.
If the run contains sustained race-pace blocks, use goal_pace_long_segments instead.
If the run contains one substantial closing segment, use fast_finish_long instead.
No separate warmup or cooldown.

metadata.name: High Volume Time Intervals
metadata.description: High-volume time-based intervals using repeated on/off segments to accumulate sustained quality work.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Minimum high-volume interval session: 15 min warmup + 12 x 90/45 sec + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{rep_count}} x {{work_duration_seconds}} sec on / {{recovery_duration_seconds}} sec off. Run the on segments at a controlled hard effort and keep the recoveries easy enough to maintain rhythm. {{cumulative_volume_instruction}}

coach_notes.intended_use: High-volume threshold-oriented interval work for well-trained half marathon and marathon athletes.
special_rules: Reserved for well-trained athletes.
Most appropriate for half marathon and marathon race cycles.
The classic version is 20 × 2 min on / 1 min off.
The cumulative workload is the stimulus.
Recoveries should usually be easy, but well-trained athletes may float them if appropriate.
Do not prescribe during recovery block or return-to-running plans.

metadata.name: Run/Walk Intervals
metadata.description: Staged run/walk session for return-to-running and insufficient-base athletes. Ten deterministic stages progress from short run segments with generous walk breaks to a first continuous run.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Run/walk is the athlete's primary session, not advanced quality work; the floor is set low so it stays eligible for quality and easy slots at typical insufficient-base weekly volumes.

display.description_template: {{run_walk_instruction}}

coach_notes.intended_use: Return-to-running progression and insufficient-base entry point. Stage is deterministic (return_to_running reads training_plans.rtr_current_stage; development insufficient-base uses a fixed early stage), so placement is not variety-seeking and the archetype is exempt from anti-repeat hard-blocking.
special_rules: Effort language only — never prescribe pace for run/walk, regardless of pace_zones_visible.
Stages 1-9 are N reps of (run X / walk Y) bookended by a brisk-walk warmup and walk cooldown.
Stage 10 is the first continuous run, warmup/cooldown folded into the easy effort.
Coach handles rehab Phases I-III outside the platform.

metadata.name: Standalone Strides
metadata.description: Short neuromuscular session: brief warmup, a handful of relaxed strides with full recovery, brief cooldown. No substantial continuous-running block, distinct from easy_with_strides.

display.description_template: A short, easy session focused on form. Warm up with {{warmup_minutes}} minutes of easy jogging or brisk walking, then run {{stride_count}} relaxed strides of about {{stride_duration_seconds}} seconds each, taking full recovery between every one. Cool down with {{cooldown_minutes}} minutes easy. Strides are smooth controlled accelerations, never sprints: focus on quick, relaxed turnover.

coach_notes.intended_use: Light neuromuscular stimulus for insufficient-base and return-to-running athletes who do not yet sustain a full easy run. Deterministic and repeatable, so it is exempt from anti-repeat hard-blocking.
special_rules: Effort language only: no pace prescription.
Full recovery between strides; quality of movement over fatigue.
Distinct from easy_with_strides, which appends strides to a full easy run.

metadata.name: Hill Sprint Ladder
metadata.description: A descending or pyramid ladder of near-maximal hill sprints, each rep a different duration, with a jog back to the bottom between every rep for neuromuscular variety in one session.

generation.minimum_duration_notes: Fixed ladder session: 15 min warmup with strides + ladder sprints with jog-back recovery + 10 min cooldown.

display.description_template: {{hill_sprint_sequence}} hill sprints. Jog back to the bottom between each rep. Each sprint is near-maximal effort regardless of duration. The variety in rep length builds neuromuscular adaptability and keeps the session engaging.

coach_notes.intended_use: Neuromuscular power and running economy with rep-length variety. Heavily weighted for ultra-distance and strength-focused athletes; light for 5K/10K/mile who already get plenty of fast work.
special_rules: Jog back to the bottom of the hill between every rep, in every variant. Never walk back, never stand and rest.
Every sprint is near-maximal effort regardless of duration.
Sequence is determined by variant (descending, pyramid, double descending).
Warmup includes 4 x 15 sec strides; cooldown is 10 min easy.
Requires hill access; substitute a safe steep hill if needed.
